profiles.name: daily-gpt55-go-provider-diverse
profiles.privacy_posture: cross-provider; avoid for sensitive repos unless accepted
profiles.cost_posture: uses GPT for high-reliability work and Go for specialist/CP lanes
profiles.recommended_for: daily local workflow when GPT-5.5 is actually executable and OpenCode Go is available
notes: Current machine profile from the originating session.

profiles.name: gpt55-oauth-openai-only
profiles.cost_posture: subscription/OAuth oriented; no OpenCode Go fallback
profiles.recommended_for: ChatGPT/Codex-style OAuth users who can run GPT-5.5 but do not want cross-provider routing
notes: Use only where openai/gpt-5.5 is locally executable; API-key availability is not assumed.

profiles.name: provider-diverse-safe-gpt54-go
profiles.routing_posture: portable OpenAI API primary, Go fallback/specialists
profiles.privacy_posture: cross-provider; avoid for sensitive repos unless accepted
profiles.recommended_for: users with OpenAI API plus OpenCode Go, without assuming GPT-5.5 API availability
notes: Portable counterpart to the GPT-5.5 daily profile.

profiles.name: go-first-openai-api-review
profiles.routing_posture: OpenCode Go first, OpenAI only as escalation/review fallback
profiles.privacy_posture: cross-provider; prompts may reach either provider
profiles.cost_posture: minimize OpenAI API spend while keeping quality escape hatches
profiles.recommended_for: users with small OpenAI API budget and cheap Go capacity
notes: Good when GPT/API quota is precious but final escalation matters.

profiles.name: openai-only-sensitive
profiles.privacy_posture: best shipped profile for sensitive/proprietary repos when OpenAI is approved
profiles.recommended_for: sensitive work where OpenCode Go or other provider fallback is not acceptable

profiles.name: openai-api-budget-mini
profiles.routing_posture: OpenAI mini-first with GPT-5.4 escalation routes
profiles.recommended_for: OpenAI API users optimizing cost for low-risk repos/tasks
notes: Keep hard-review categories on GPT-5.4; do not use as final authority for high-risk changes.

profiles.name: opencode-go-only-balanced
profiles.recommended_for: users who only have OpenCode Go and want a general-purpose setup

profiles.name: opencode-go-only-low-cost
profiles.routing_posture: cheap sweep first, stronger Go models only as fallback
profiles.cost_posture: lowest Go spend / highest request budget
profiles.recommended_for: bulk summarization, classification, exploration, low-risk docs
notes: Not recommended as final reviewer for complex code.

profiles.name: opencode-go-only-large-context
profiles.cost_posture: spend Go quota where huge context matters
profiles.recommended_for: large repo sweeps, big logs, long documents, context-heavy audits
notes: Prefer only when context size is the binding constraint.

profiles.name: opencode-go-only-multimodal
profiles.cost_posture: spend Go quota where image/audio/video/PDF context matters
notes: Use balanced Go profile for ordinary text/code work.

profiles.name: google-gemini-only
access: Google AI/Gemini API or Antigravity auth
profiles.recommended_for: users with Gemini/Antigravity access but no OpenAI/OpenCode Go
notes: Model IDs were locally listed; smoke-test in your environment.

profiles.name: google-gemini-go-provider-diverse
access: Google AI/Gemini API or Antigravity auth
profiles.cost_posture: use Gemini for reasoning and Go for context/multimodal/cheap sweeps
profiles.recommended_for: Gemini-first users who also have Go for CP/context lanes
notes: Good alternative when OpenAI is unavailable or quota constrained.

profiles.name: cursor-local-router-only
access: Cursor subscription with local router/ACP adapter
profiles.privacy_posture: Cursor boundary only; cursor and cursor-acp are local-router transports, not separate provider fallbacks
profiles.recommended_for: users who want OpenCode UI/routing over an existing Cursor subscription
notes: Requires the local Cursor-compatible endpoint to be running.

profiles.name: cursor-go-provider-diverse
access: Cursor subscription with local router/ACP adapter
profiles.cost_posture: use Cursor subscription for core work and Go for CP/context/cheap lanes
profiles.recommended_for: Cursor users who also have OpenCode Go and want cheaper/faster side lanes
notes: Requires the local Cursor-compatible endpoint to be running.
